Title: Correct backlog & User Story creation
Steps (Action => Expected): Open the **client-specific** Jira backlog (confirm with product owner/PM) => Correct backlog | Create a new User Story (not Task/Bug; not in a generic backlog) => Story created
Pass/Fail Criteria: Story created in the correct client backlog.

Title: Title formatted with real values, no placeholders
Steps (Action => Expected): Apply the phase's Title template => Matches exactly | Replace every `REF[...]` with the value from Env Variables "Key" column => All replaced | Search the story for literal "REF[" => Zero matches
Pass/Fail Criteria: No unresolved `REF[]` anywhere in Title/Description/AC.

Title: Description references the Tier from Phase 1
Steps (Action => Expected): Confirm the Tier cited equals the Phase 1 Env Variables Tier => Identical | Confirm the correct Tier architecture file is the one referenced => Matches Tier
Pass/Fail Criteria: Tier in ticket = Phase 1 Tier; correct Tier spec linked. (Skip if phase has no Tier reference.)

Title: All reference links valid
Steps (Action => Expected): Follow every link in the Description (architecture folder, windward-1.0/2.0, Env Variables, phase-specific docs) => All resolve | Confirm linked docs are the intended targets => Correct targets
Pass/Fail Criteria: No broken or wrong links.

Title: Acceptance Criteria matches playbook wording
Steps (Action => Expected): Enter the phase's prescribed Acceptance Criteria => Present and saved | Confirm it states the measurable done-condition => Yes
Pass/Fail Criteria: AC present, accurate, saved.

Title: Ticket assigned to the correct specialist team
Steps (Action => Expected): Assign per the phase Work Assignments => Correct team/owner | Confirm not left unassigned / wrong team => Correct
Pass/Fail Criteria: Routed to the documented team.

Title: One ticket per env, DEV→QAR→PROD→HFX
Steps (Action => Expected): If the phase processes per-environment, create one ticket per env => Separate tickets | Wait for each env to complete before the next => Order honored
Pass/Fail Criteria: Sequential order respected. (Skip if single-shot phase.)

Title: Completion confirmed by specialist team
Steps (Action => Expected): After assignment, monitor ticket => Specialist team updates to Done/Resolved or comments completion | Confirm the documented Output/Completion-Checklist deliverables are met => Deliverables evidenced in ticket
Pass/Fail Criteria: Specialist team confirms completion with evidence; otherwise follow up.

Title: Phase blocked if prior-phase deliverables incomplete
Steps (Action => Expected): Attempt the phase with an unmet prerequisite (e.g., prior phase not COMPLETE, missing access) => Cannot proceed; gap surfaced
Pass/Fail Criteria: Unmet prerequisite blocks the phase rather than producing an incomplete ticket.

Title: All required Client Information fields collected
Objective: Verify every Client Information field is gathered from the correct stakeholder and recorded before Step 2.
Steps (Action => Expected): Collect Tenant Name, Tenant ID, Single/Multi-Tenant, Tenant Country Code, Client Member Count => All six fields present and non-empty | Confirm each value's source matches the "Request data from" column => Architect/Coordinator for tenant fields; AJ's SQL or NBI for member count | Confirm values temporarily recorded (notes/email) before Step 2 => Evidence exists
Pass/Fail Criteria: All fields collected from the documented source and recorded. Missing any field = Fail.

Title: All required AWS Account Information fields collected
Objective: Verify Cost Center, ATG, and New/Existing role are gathered from the correct owners.
Steps (Action => Expected): Collect Cost Center (Finance — Kelly Barrero) => 6-digit code obtained | Collect ATG (Architect) => ATG governance ID obtained (e.g., ATG0004932) | Collect New vs Existing IAM role decision (Cloud Infra — Erik/Lindsay) => Decision recorded
Pass/Fail Criteria: All three AWS fields obtained from documented owners.

Title: Field-format validation
Objective: Verify format rules are enforced; malformed inputs are rejected.
Steps (Action => Expected): Enter Tenant ID = "KCL" (3 chars) => Accepted | Enter Tenant ID = "KC" or "KCLM" => Rejected — must be exactly 3 chars | Enter Tenant ID equal to an existing tenant's ID => Rejected — must be unique across all tenants | Enter Cost Center = "12AB56" or "1234" => Rejected — must be 6 digits | Enter Country Code = "USA" => Rejected — expected short code "us"/"ca"
Pass/Fail Criteria: Each malformed value is caught before Step 2.

Title: Existing vs New client member-count path
Objective: Verify the correct member-count source is used per client type.
Steps (Action => Expected): Client exists in Windward → run `ClientMemCount.sql` on PROD => Count obtained via SQL (see `TC_SQL_ClientMemCount`) | Client NOT yet in Windward → NBI process => Flagged as gap G1 (NBI = TBD); onboarding blocked until NBI defined
Pass/Fail Criteria: Correct path chosen; new-client gap surfaced rather than silently skipped.

Title: Member count produced by authorized US-based operator
Objective: Verify PHI-touching member count is sourced compliantly.
Steps (Action => Expected): Identify who ran the PROD count => A US-based team member with authorized PROD/PHI access | Attempt to source count from a non-US / unauthorized operator => Rejected / not permitted
Pass/Fail Criteria: Count provenance is compliant; otherwise Fail (S1 security defect).

Title: Tab copy & filter procedure
Objective: Verify the operator copies `Master_Sheet`, renames to client, and filters to Phase 1 / Manual rows.
Steps (Action => Expected): Copy `Master_Sheet` tab in Environment Variables workbook => New tab created | Rename tab to client name (e.g., "Client X") => Tab renamed | Filter Col H = Phase 1, Col G = Manual => Only Phase 1 Manual rows shown | Clear existing Col C values on those rows => Manual cells emptied, ready for input
Pass/Fail Criteria: Working tab prepared exactly per instructions; `Master_Sheet` left unmodified.

Title: Tier derived correctly at every boundary
Objective: Verify Tier logic: `>=1,000,000 → Tier 1`, `>=100,000 → Tier 2`, `<100,000 → Tier 3`. This is the single highest-value computed value in Phase 1.
Pass/Fail Criteria: Every row yields the expected Tier. Any boundary miss = Fail (S1 — wrong Tier mis-sizes all downstream infrastructure).

Title: All Manual Phase-1 fields entered in Column C
Objective: Verify every Manual field is populated: Single/Multi-Tenant, Member Count, Tier, Tenant Name, Tenant ID, Country Code, Cost Center, ATG, New/Existing role.
Steps (Action => Expected): Inspect Col C for each Manual row => All nine values present, none left blank | Cross-check each value vs Step 1 collected data => Exact match
Pass/Fail Criteria: All Manual fields populated and consistent with Step 1.

Title: Spreadsheet values match stakeholder source exactly
Objective: Verify no transcription drift between stakeholder inputs and spreadsheet.
Steps (Action => Expected): Compare each Col C value to the recorded stakeholder value => Character-for-character match | Compare formats to Step 1 rules => All formats valid
Pass/Fail Criteria: Zero discrepancies.

Title: Two operators, same client → identical Tier & values
Objective: Verify the procedure is deterministic.
Steps (Action => Expected): Operator A and Operator B independently complete Phase 1 for C-MID => Same Member Count, same Tier 2, same field values
Pass/Fail Criteria: Results identical across operators.

Title: Links, references, and glossary consistency
Steps (Action => Expected): Follow every `[...](...)` link in Phase 1 => All resolve (Introduction, Glossary, ClientMemCount.sql, Env Variables) | Confirm every defined term (Tenant, Tier, Single/Multi-Tenant, etc.) exists in Glossary => All present and non-contradictory | Confirm Completion Checklist items map to Steps 1–2 outputs => Each deliverable traceable
Pass/Fail Criteria: No broken links, no undefined/contradictory terms.

Title: Golden client returns verified count
Objective: Query result matches the independently verified member count for the golden client.
Steps (Action => Expected): Set `purchaser_gid = <GOLD>`; run on PROD => Executes without error | `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM #temp_member_search` => Equals GOLD expected count | Compare to independent source (NBI/prior audit) => Match within agreed tolerance (ideally exact)
Pass/Fail Criteria: Count matches verified figure. Mismatch = Fail (S1).

Title: No duplicate members inflate the count
Objective: `contact_relation_gid` is distinct despite multiple coverage rows per member.
Steps (Action => Expected): `SELECT COUNT(*) , COUNT(DISTINCT contact_relation_gid) FROM #temp_member_search` => Both equal | Confirm `GROUP BY cm.contact_relation_gid` collapses multi-coverage members => One row per member
Pass/Fail Criteria: No duplicates. (The `GROUP BY` should guarantee this — verify it actually does given the multi-join fan-out.)

Title: Total vs Active count clarified
Objective: Phase 1 asks to record **both total and active** counts, but this query returns ONE number filtered by `record_status='A'` on every join. Verify what the number actually represents.
Steps (Action => Expected): Determine semantics of `record_status='A'` => 'A' = row is active/not-deleted, NOT member coverage status | Check whether termed (coverage-ended) members are included => Per header intent, termed-but-resident members SHOULD be counted | Confirm whether a separate "active members" figure exists => If none → **defect**: query provides total only; "active count" undefined
Pass/Fail Criteria: Semantics documented; if no active-only query exists, raise defect against Phase 1 / SQL (gap G2). Do not guess the active figure.

Title: Termed members are counted
Objective: Validate the header claim that inactive/termed members still in Windward are included.
Steps (Action => Expected): Run for TERMED profile (members termed but data resident) => Count > 0 | Cross-check a known termed member appears in `#temp_member_search` => Present
Pass/Fail Criteria: Termed-but-resident members included as intended. (Note: if `record_status='A'` joins exclude them, this contradicts the header — raise defect.)

Title: Count feeds correct Tier
Objective: The produced count maps to the Tier rule (link to `TC-P1-07`).
Steps (Action => Expected): Take query count and apply Tier rule => >=1M→T1, >=100K→T2, <100K→T3 | Verify count near a Tier boundary classifies correctly => Correct Tier
Pass/Fail Criteria: Count → Tier mapping correct.

Title: Nonexistent purchaser_gid
Steps (Action => Expected): Set `purchaser_gid = 999999999`; run => 0 rows; no error | Operator interprets 0 correctly => Recognized as "no members / wrong gid", not Tier 3 by default
Pass/Fail Criteria: Empty result handled and interpreted, not silently taken as a real count.

Title: Read-only, no side effects
Objective: Verify the script only creates a temp table and reads; it must not alter production data.
Steps (Action => Expected): Review statements => Only `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #temp_member_search` (session temp) + `SELECT ... INTO #temp` | Confirm `#temp_member_search` is session-scoped (single `#`) => Yes — no global/permanent table impact | Run with read-only credentials => Succeeds; no write to base tables
Pass/Fail Criteria: No production data modified; temp table is session-scoped.

Title: Re-run yields identical count
Steps (Action => Expected): Run twice in succession for GOLD => Identical counts (barring real-time enrollment changes) | Confirm `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS` makes the script safely re-runnable in one session => No "object exists" error on re-run
Pass/Fail Criteria: Deterministic and re-runnable.

Title: Instructions are followable
Steps (Action => Expected): Follow header instructions (replace gid, run on PROD, new-client note) => Clear and sufficient | Note: header says "Replace 'purchaser_gid'" but code hardcodes `p.purchaser_gid = 86319` => **Defect**: instruction/code mismatch — the literal to replace is the numeric value, not a placeholder named `purchaser_gid`
Pass/Fail Criteria: Operator can run it unaided; doc/code mismatch logged as minor defect.

Title: Runs within acceptable window on PROD
Steps (Action => Expected): Time execution for a large (Tier 1) client => Completes within agreed window; no PROD impact | Confirm no table locks/blocking on base tables => Read-only, minimal footprint
Pass/Fail Criteria: Acceptable runtime, no production disruption.

Objective: Verify the request is raised via the documented catalog path.
Steps (Action => Expected): Service Catalog → "Are you requesting something new?" → Infrastructure Request => Form opens | Type of Request = "Cloud Request" => Selected | Cloud Provider = "AWS" => Selected | Short Description = "AWS Accounts Request" => Entered
Pass/Fail Criteria: Correct catalog item and field selections reached.

Pass/Fail Criteria: No unresolved `REF[]` remains. Any leftover = Fail (S2).

Steps (Action => Expected): Compare each ticket value to spreadsheet Column C–F => Character-for-character match | Confirm no extra/missing fields => All 11 keys present
Pass/Fail Criteria: Zero discrepancies before submission.

Steps (Action => Expected): Confirm Description includes the `/architecture/kerberos-setup` link (not just generic arch) => Present & resolves

Pass/Fail Criteria: Operator's submit/skip choice matches the expected decision for each scenario. Wrong choice = Fail (either wasted intake or unallocated InfoSec).
